title: Discrete-Time Survival Model For Remaining Laps To Next Pit
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem, not just a one-step classification problem.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem, not just a one-step classification problem. Discrete-time survival lets you learn a hazard curve over upcoming laps and then extract the next-lap hazard as the submission target, while enforcing consistency across 1, 2, 3+ lap horizons. That is a different direction from adding more local tyre heuristics.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race sequence, define event time as laps until next `PitStop==1`; censor at sequence end if no future stop. Expand to person-period rows for horizons 1..H or use a survival forest / discrete-time hazard model. Train with features at time t plus horizon index, then use predicted hazard at horizon 1 as `PitNextLap`. Compare to a multi-horizon classifier sharing parameters.
expected_effect: Should improve late-stint timing and reduce inconsistent probabilities across adjacent laps.
risk: Data expansion can be large and CV leakage becomes easier if horizons from the same sequence cross folds; keep splits sequence-pure.
